skill.name: payment-integration
skill.description: Integrate Stripe, PayPal, and payment processors. Handles checkout flows, subscriptions, webhooks, and PCI compliance.
skill.summary: Guides secure payment processor integration for checkout, subscriptions, webhooks, and billing operations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and were determined to be false positives. The file contains payment integration guidance and markdown formatting, with no executable commands, weak cryptography, network reconnaissance, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 25 and 50 use backticks for inline markdown references and status text. They do not execute shell commands or instruct command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks wrap prose values, not Ruby or shell syntax. No command invocation, interpolation, or user input execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Has No Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 3 is a plain description of payment processors and checkout flows. It does not mention or implement any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains descriptive metadata only. No weak hash, cipher, key handling, or crypto implementation appears at that location.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Finding Is Contextual Guidance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 61 describes a payment processor outage scenario. It does not request scanning, probing, enumeration, or unauthorized network activity.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is a business continuity example for payment systems. It contains no reconnaissance instructions or tooling references.

content.value_statement: Payment systems are hard to implement safely because checkout, subscriptions, and webhooks have many edge cases. This skill gives structured guidance for secure processor integrations.
actual_capabilities: Guides Stripe, PayPal, and Square integration planning.
Explains checkout flows, payment forms, and subscription billing.
Provides webhook security guidance for signatures and idempotency.
Covers PCI basics, tokenization, and raw card data avoidance.
Outlines payment failure handling, refunds, disputes, and retries.
Suggests test scenarios and production migration checks.
limitations: Does not include provider credentials or live account setup.
Does not replace legal, PCI, or financial compliance review.
Requires current provider documentation for exact API details.
Does not execute payments or verify real gateway configuration.

use_cases.title: Launch Checkout
use_cases.description: Plan a secure checkout flow with tokenized card handling and server-side validation.

use_cases.title: Harden Webhooks
use_cases.description: Review webhook handlers for signature verification, raw body handling, and idempotent event processing.

use_cases.title: Improve Billing Reliability
use_cases.description: Map subscription, retry, refund, dispute, and failure paths before production release.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Payment Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a secure payment flow for my product. Ask for the business model, payment methods, and region.

prompt_templates.title: Design Checkout Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Stripe or PayPal checkout implementation. Include client flow, server validation, tokenization, and test cases.

prompt_templates.title: Review Webhook Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my webhook design for signature verification, raw body preservation, idempotency, retries, and provider status checks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced team preparing a launch review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production readiness review for payments. Cover secrets, environments, monitoring, disputes, refunds, compliance, and failure recovery.

output_examples.input: Review a new subscription billing feature before launch.
output: A launch checklist covering checkout, recurring billing, webhook events, retries, refunds, and customer notifications.
A security review focused on tokenization, server validation, signature checks, and environment separation.
A test plan for successful payments, failed payments, cancellations, disputes, and webhook replay.

output_examples.input: Design webhook handling for payment events.
output: A handler design that verifies provider signatures before processing events.
An idempotency plan using stored event identifiers to prevent duplicate work.
A queue and retry approach that responds quickly while processing slow tasks safely.

best_practices: Use official provider SDKs and hosted card collection where possible.
Verify every webhook signature before processing payment events.
Store event identifiers and make payment operations idempotent.
anti_patterns: Do not store, log, or transmit raw card numbers.
Do not trust client-side payment status without server verification.
Do not skip test mode coverage before enabling production payments.

faq.question: Does this skill process payments directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides integration guidance and checklists for implementing payment systems with provider tools.

faq.question: Which payment providers does it cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on Stripe, PayPal, Square, and general processor patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with PCI compliance?
faq.answer: It gives practical PCI-oriented guidance, but it does not replace formal compliance review.

faq.question: Does it cover webhook security?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes signature verification, raw body preservation, idempotency, and provider-side status checks.

faq.question: Is it useful for subscriptions?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers recurring billing, failed payments, refunds, disputes, and retry behavior.

faq.question: Should I still read provider documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. Provider APIs change, so always confirm exact implementation details with official documentation.
